topStories.headline: Festa Italiana Returns to North Beach This Weekend With 105th Statuto Race
topStories.dek: San Francisco's only Italian street festival takes over Stockton Street June 6–7 with food, music, pizza tossing, and the historic Statuto Race.
topStories.body:
North Beach's beloved Festa Italiana, also known as the Festa Coloniale Italiana, returns this weekend on Saturday and Sunday, June 6–7. The San Francisco Italian Athletic Club Foundation hosts the free, family-friendly celebration of Italian food, wine, music, and culture on Stockton Street between Union and Filbert streets, from 11 a.m. to 6 p.m. both days.

Highlights include nonstop live Italian music, a pizza toss performance by 13-time World Champion and restaurateur Tony Gemignani of Tony's Pizza Napoletana, Italian cuisine and wine tasting, a kids' corner with tarantella dancing lessons, and an Italian vendor faire featuring local favorites such as Mozzeria, Graffeo, and Poesia Cafe.

The centerpiece of Sunday morning is the 105th running of the Statuto Race, one of North Beach's most storied athletic traditions. Registration is open through the SFIAC Foundation.

SFMTA has announced that Stockton Street between Union and Filbert will be closed to through traffic from 4 a.m. Saturday to 10 p.m. Sunday, and Muni's 39 Coit line will reroute around the event for all hours of service. Riders should consult SFMTA's website for details on affected stops.

topStories.headline: New Fisherman's Wharf Public Plaza Nears Summer Opening as Port Seeks Input on Long-Term Resilience
topStories.dek: The 5,000-square-foot Taylor Street plaza where Alioto's once stood is on track to open this summer, while the Port gathers community feedback on seismic and flood protections.
topStories.body:
The new public plaza at the heart of Fisherman's Wharf is on track for a summer 2026 opening, according to the SF Examiner. The 5,000-square-foot space on Taylor Street occupies the site where the shuttered Alioto's restaurant — a Wharf landmark for nearly a century — once stood. The building was demolished as part of the $10 million Fisherman's Wharf Forward initiative.

The plaza will feature wave-shaped seating, picnic tables, string lights, and sweeping views of the Golden Gate Bridge, San Francisco Bay, and the fishing boats of the inner lagoon. An additional inner lagoon overlook along Al Scoma Way and upgraded lighting to highlight the fishing fleet are also part of the near-term improvements.

Meanwhile, as reported by the SF Examiner on May 3, the Port of San Francisco has entered the next phase of the project — a roughly four-month community engagement process to shape long-term seismic and flood resilience goals for the Wharf. Some community members have launched an effort to name the new plaza after the Alioto family, which first came to San Francisco over 100 years ago.

The timing of the plaza opening aligns with FIFA World Cup 2026, which begins June 11 at Bay Area venues. PIER 39 has announced it will host free outdoor World Cup watch parties — dubbed "Soccer with the Sea Lions" — on a big screen at the bay end of the pier for select matches throughout the tournament.

topStories.headline: Escape From Alcatraz Triathlon Takes Over the Waterfront This Weekend
topStories.dek: The 46th-year iconic triathlon sends athletes plunging from a boat near Alcatraz Island on Sunday, June 7, with a free fitness festival at Marina Green.
topStories.body:
The Escape From Alcatraz Triathlon returns to San Francisco's waterfront this weekend for its 46th year, with the pro race on Saturday, June 6, and the legendary amateur triathlon on Sunday, June 7. The event features a daring 1.5-mile swim from waters near Alcatraz Island, an 18-mile bike ride through the Presidio and Golden Gate Park, and a grueling 8-mile run including the famous Sand Ladder at Baker Beach.

The race draws thousands of athletes from all 50 states and over 50 countries. This year's edition has drawn heightened attention to safety following an incident at the 2025 race in which a participant was seriously injured during the swim start. The 2026 athlete guide devotes a full page to jump procedures, and organizers have emphasized updated safety protocols, according to Triathlete magazine.

North Waterfront residents and visitors can enjoy the free Fitness Festival at Marina Green on both days — Saturday from 9 a.m. to 2:30 p.m. and Sunday from 7 a.m. to 2:30 p.m. — featuring merchandise, a beer garden, local food trucks, and sponsor activations. Expect road closures and heavy spectator traffic along the Embarcadero, Marina Boulevard, and through the Presidio on race morning.

events.title: Festa Coloniale Italiana (Festa Italiana)
events.location: Stockton Street between Union and Filbert, North Beach
events.description: Free Italian street festival featuring live music, pizza toss by Tony Gemignani, wine tasting, Italian vendors, kids' activities, and the 105th Statuto Race on Sunday morning.

events.title: Escape From Alcatraz Triathlon & Fitness Festival
events.time: 6:00 AM (race); Festival 9:00 AM–2:30 PM Sat, 7:00 AM–2:30 PM Sun
events.location: Marina Green / San Francisco Bay waterfront
events.description: The 46th-year iconic triathlon with a 1.5-mile swim from Alcatraz, 18-mile bike, and 8-mile run. Free Fitness Festival open to the public at Marina Green both days.

events.title: Soccer with the Sea Lions: FIFA World Cup Watch Parties at PIER 39
events.location: PIER 39, Bay End big screen, Fisherman's Wharf
events.description: Free outdoor screenings of select FIFA World Cup 2026 matches at the bay end of PIER 39, including Team USA games. Schedule runs through July 19.

events.title: 72nd Annual North Beach Festival
events.location: Grant Avenue and Columbus Avenue, North Beach
events.description: One of the country's original outdoor festivals returns with 200+ vendors on 11 blocks, beer gardens, live music, Italian street painting, the Blessing of the Animals, and a kids' Fun Zone. Free admission.

events.title: Dear San Francisco — Immersive Circus Show
events.location: Club Fugazi, 678 Green Street, North Beach
events.description: The critically acclaimed acrobatic circus show by The 7 Fingers celebrates San Francisco's history and spirit in an intimate 300-seat venue. Tickets from $86.90.

events.title: Juneteenth on the Waterfront
events.description: Sixth annual celebration of local Black-owned businesses on the waterfront, coinciding with Juneteenth commemorations across the city.

events.title: Critical Mass Bike Ride
events.description: The monthly mass bicycle ride departs from the Embarcadero. Open to all cyclists.

moreNews.headline: PIER 39 Designated Official FIFA World Cup 2026 Watch Party Venue
moreNews.body: PIER 39 at Fisherman's Wharf is one of more than 30 official Bay Area venues hosting free FIFA World Cup 2026 watch parties, the Bay Area Host Committee announced. The venue will broadcast select matches — including Team USA group-stage games, knockout rounds, and the Final — on a big screen at the bay end of the pier, beginning June 12. The World Cup arrives at Bay Area venues starting June 11, with six matches at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara.

moreNews.headline: Wharf's Pop-Up Fish Market Thrives as Season Shifts from Crab to Salmon
moreNews.body: The weekly pop-up fish market at Al Scoma Way in Fisherman's Wharf, which launched in January 2026, has been a success, averaging 77 customers per week with a high of 117, according to minutes from the March 4 Fisherman's Wharf Advisory Committee meeting. The market operates alongside off-the-boat crab sales at Wharf J9. The Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District reports that with the fishing season transitioning from Dungeness crab to salmon, the market hopes to sustain its momentum through summer.
moreNews.sourceName: Port of San Francisco / FWAC Minutes

moreNews.headline: Fisherman's Wharf Entertainment Zone Approved for Open-Air Drinks
moreNews.body: The Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District's Entertainment Zone has received city approval, according to the FWAC meeting minutes. The zone — a City of San Francisco designation allowing open-air alcohol consumption — spans the area between Polk and Powell streets and from Beach Street to the Little Embarcadero. The FWCBD planned to launch its first event in the new zone with an annual chowder competition.
moreNews.sourceName: Port of San Francisco / FWAC Minutes

shops.name: PIER 39 Shops
